# Server-Side

Tealium server-side products help you manage and enrich incoming data, create visitor profiles, manage audiences, and orchestrate outgoing-data with storage solutions that let you combine data collected from web and mobile touchpoints, online and offline channels, and real-time audiences.

## About markdown versions

All pages in this area are available in clean markdown format. To access the markdown version of any page, append `/index.md` to the page URL.

Example:
- HTML: `https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/what-is-tealium-iq/`
- Markdown: `https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/what-is-tealium-iq/index.md`


## Server-Side


- [Introduction to AudienceStream](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/introduction/): AudienceStream is an Omnichannel customer segmentation and real-time action engine.

- [Introduction to EventStream](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/introduction/): This article is an introduction to Tealium EventStream, a data collection and API hub that sits at the center of your data supply chain.

- [Audiencestream tutorial](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/tutorial/): This guide introduces you to Tealium AudienceStream, the application interface and the fundamental concepts of a customer data platform (CDP).

- [Tealium EventStream tutorial](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/tutorial/): This guide introduces you to Tealium EventStream and helps you understand the application interface and the fundamental concepts of event data management.

- [About arrays](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/arrays/about/): This article describes how to use the data types array of strings, array of numbers, and array of booleans.

- [Affirm incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/affirm/): This article describes how to use the Affirm data source to create a webhook in your Affirm account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Airship incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/airship/): This article describes how to use the Airship data source to create a webhook in your Airship account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Array of booleans](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/arrays/array-of-booleans/): This article describes how to use an array of booleans.

- [Array of numbers](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/arrays/array-of-numbers/): This article describes how to use the data types array of strings, array of numbers, and array of booleans.

- [Array of strings](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/arrays/arrays/): This article describes how to use the data types array of strings, array of numbers, and array of booleans.

- [Auth0 incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/auth0/): This article describes how to use the Auth0 data source to create a webhook in your Auth0 account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Badge Attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/badge/): A badge is a special visitor attribute for capturing the interesting behavior of a customer.

- [Boolean Attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/boolean/): This article explains how to use the boolean attribute data type. Booleans are used to store true or false values.

- [Braze Currents data source setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/braze-currents/): This article describes how to use Braze as a data source to send actionable events to Tealium EventStream API Hub and Tealium AudienceStream CDP using Braze Currents.

- [Date Attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/date/): The date attribute stores the timestamp of an event.

- [Funnel attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/funnel/): This article describes the funnel data type and how to use it.

- [Google Ads Leads setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/google-ads-leads/): This article provides information on adding a Google Ads Leads data source and adding a webhook for this data source.

- [Google BigQuery cloud data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/google-bigquery-cloud-data-source/): This article describes how to set up the Google BigQuery cloud data source.

- [HTTP API - Advanced incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/http-api-advanced/): This article describes how to use the HTTP API - Advanced data source.

- [Hubspot Workflow incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/hubspot/): This article describes how to use the Hubspot data source to create a webhook in your Hubspot account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Insider data source setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/insider/): This article describes how to use Insider as a data source to send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Install options for web](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/install/): This article describes the installation options for Tealium AudienceStream CDP.

- [Intercom incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/intercom/): This article describes how to use the Intercom data source to create a webhook in your Intercom account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Iterable incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/iterable/): This article describes how to use the Iterable data source to create a webhook in your Iterable account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Mailchimp incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/mailchimp/): This article describes how to use the Mailchimp data source to create a webhook in your Mailchimp account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Tealium Tools Mobile QR Trace](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/trace/mobile-qr-trace/): The Mobile Trace Tool enhances the Trace feature by providing a QR code that is scannable by your device, enabling you to quickly view events coming from the Tealium Mobile SDKs.

- [Number Attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/number/): This article describes how to use the number attribute.

- [Tealium Tool Optimizely Helper](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/optimizely-helper/): Installing Tealium Tools Make sure you have the latest version of Tealium Tools installed. This Google Chrome extension provides a variety of tools, …

- [Popular Badges](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/popular-badges/): These are popular Badges that have proven useful to other clients.

- [PulsePoint HCP365 incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/pulsepoint-hcp365/): This article describes how to use the PulsePoint HCP365 data source to create a webhook in your PulsePoint account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [SendGrid incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/sendgrid/): This article describes how to use the SendGrid data source to create a webhook in your SendGrid account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [Set of Strings Attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/set-of-strings/): The set of strings attribute stores a set of unique text values.

- [String Attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/string/): Use string attributes to store text values.

- [Tally Attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/tally/): This article explains how to use the tally attribute.

- [Timeline Attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-types/timeline/): This article describes the timeline attribute and how to use it.

- [Webhook - Keen.io API](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/vendor-examples/webhook-keenio-api/): How to configure an advanced Webhook - Send Custom Request action using Template Variables for Keen.io API

- [Webhook - Kony API](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/vendor-examples/webhook-kony-api/): How to configure an advanced Webhook - Send Custom Request action using Template Variables for Kony API

- [Webhook Facebook App Events API](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/vendor-examples/webhook-facebook-app-events-api/): How to configure the Facebook App Events API using the Webhook - Send Custom Request action with Templates and Template Variables.

- [Webhook Krux (Deprecated)](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/vendor-examples/webhook-krux-deprecated/): The Krux Webhook is a deprecated feature and has been replaced with the Salesforce Audience Studio Connector.
Prerequisite:
Webhook Send Custom Action …

- [Webhook Salesforce Predictive Intelligence API](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/vendor-examples/webhook-salesforce-predictive-intelligence-api/): How to configure an advanced Webhook - Send Custom Request action using Template Variables for Salesforce Predictive Intelligence API

- [Zapier incoming webhook setup guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/webhooks/setup-guides/zapier/): This article describes how to use the Zapier data source to create a webhook in your Zapier account and send actionable events to Tealium.

- [About Data Layer Enrichment Public API](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-layer-enrichment/data-layer-enrichment-api/data-layer-enrichment-public-api/): The Data Layer Enrichment Public API lets you retrieve profile data for an active visitor.

- [About cloud data sources](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/about/): This article describes how to import data from a cloud datawarehouse or database.

- [About Moments API](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/moments-api/about/): Moments API is a high-performance, configurable endpoint that retrieves a targeted slice of live visitor context for real-time personalization, AI systems, and cross-channel experiences.

- [About file import](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/about/): This article describes how to import CSV files as a data source, allowing you to enrich offline data and make it actionable within the Customer Data Hub.

- [Introduction to Customer Data Hub](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/intro-cdh/): The Customer Data Hub is a single approach to collecting and managing data and making it available to every customer touch-point and marketing technology vendor in real time and fuels exceptional customer experiences and insights.

- [Upload files to a service](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/file-transfer-service/upload-files/): This article describes how to get started uploading files to your file service.

- [About audiences](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/about/): This article provides information about audiences, segments, and visitor retention time.

- [About enrichments](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/enrichments/enrichments/): An enrichment is the use of custom logic to transform an attribute from a static value to a dynamic one. This is used to create new data values or …

- [About functions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/about/): Functions are small blocks of JavaScript code that you can use to extend Tealium functionality. This article provides information on function types and the key benefits of functions.

- [Use Cyberduck to upload a file with SFTP or Amazon S3](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/file-transfer-service/cyberduck/): This article describes how to configure Cyberduck to upload a file with SFTP or Amazon S3.

- [Prepare a CSV file for import](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/prepare-a-csv-file/): This article helps you prepare your files for a successful import using the file import data source.

- [About CloudStream](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/cloudstream/about/): This article provides information about CloudStream.

- [About Data Connect](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/about/): This article provides an overview of Tealium Data Connect.

- [Snowflake cloud data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/snowflake-cloud-data-source/about/): This article provides specific details about setting up the Snowflake cloud data source.

- [About Tealium Audience Discovery for Snowflake](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/snowflake-cloud-data-source/tealium-audience-discovery/about/): Overview of Tealium Audience Discovery for Snowflake, its capabilities, and how it integrates with Tealium for audience activation.

- [About AudienceDB and EventDB](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencedb-eventdb/about/): This article describes how to work with AudienceDB and EventDB.

- [About attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/about/): This article provides an overview of how attributes are used and steps to add, duplicate, or remove an attribute.

- [About connector templates](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/templates/about/): Learn how to build advanced connector requests using templates.

- [About connectors](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/about/): This article describes how to add and configure connectors for Tealium EventStream API Hub and Tealium AudienceStream CDP.

- [About Data Layer Enrichment](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-layer-enrichment/about/): Data Layer Enrichment makes AudienceStream visit and visitor attributes available in your Tealium iQ configuration and populates that data in your data layer.

- [About data sources](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/about-data-sources/): This article describes the different types of data sources that you can use to send data to Tealium.

- [About event and visitor functions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/about/): This article provides information on event and visitor functions.

- [About event health](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/event-health/about/): This article explains event health and its components, and how it helps you monitor and improve data quality.

- [About Data Connect integrations](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/about-integrations/): To enable Data Connect and begin creating and managing recipes, contact support.
Data Connect uses Workato to create automated, low-code integrations …

- [About visitor stitching](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/about/): Visitor stitching is a form of identity resolution that works across platforms, devices, and browsers to associate activity to the same visitor. This article describes how visitor stitching works, how to use the visitor ID attribute effectively, and best practices.

- [About webhook connectors](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/about/): Connect the webhook connector to an HTTP API endpoint to send event or visitor data directly to your service, either by posting the full JSON payload or mapping individual attributes to request fields.

- [Add an enrichment](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/enrichments/add-enrichment/): This article describes how to add an enrichment.

- [Use the Amazon Command Line Interface to upload and manage files](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/file-transfer-service/aws-cli/): This article describes how to use the AWS Command Line Interface to upload and manage files.

- [Audience dashboard](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiencestream-dashboard/): This article explains the features of the AudienceStream Dashboard.

- [AWS S3](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/file-transfer-service/aws-s3/): This article describes how to use the AWS S3 bucket to upload and manage files.

- [Azure storage](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/file-transfer-service/azure/): This article describes how to use the Azure Blob and Azure File Storage to upload and manage files.

- [Function triggers](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/triggers/): This article provides information on triggers for each function type.

- [Manage a cloud data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/manage/): This article describes how to manage a cloud data source.

- [Data Layer Enrichment API objects](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-layer-enrichment/data-layer-enrichment-api/dle-objects/): The Data Layer Enrichment API object has the following properties that correspond to AudienceStream attributes:
Property AudienceStream attribute …

- [SFTP](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/file-transfer-service/sftp/): This article describes how to use an SFTP connection to upload and manage files.

- [Tealium S3 Bucket](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/file-transfer-service/tealium-s3/): This article describes how to use a Tealium S3 bucket to upload and manage files.

- [Session length](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/session-length/): This article explains visit session length in AudienceStream.

- [Get started with Tealium Audience Discovery for Snowflake](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/snowflake-cloud-data-source/tealium-audience-discovery/get-started/): Set up Tealium Audience Discovery for Snowflake and connect your first audience to Tealium.

- [Encryption and compression in File Import data sources](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/encryption-and-compression/): This article describes how compression and encryption options work for a file import data source.

- [Preloaded attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/preloaded/): This article describes preloaded attributes.

- [About enrichment rules](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/enrichments/about-enrichment-rules/): This article explains how rules work in attribute enrichments and when to apply them for event, visit, and visitor attributes.

- [Webhook authentication](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/authentication/): This article describes how to configure different authentication methods for the HTTP-based webhook connector.

- [Data Connect recipe best practices](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/best-practices/): This article describes best practices for Data Connect.

- [Enable Data Layer Enrichment](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-layer-enrichment/enable/): This article describes how to enable Data Layer Enrichment.

- [Execution environment and rate limits](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/environment/): This article provides information on the execution environment, rate limits, and execution limits for functions.

- [File import configuration](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/configuration/): This article describes how to configure your file import data source.

- [Workato glossary](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/glossary/): This article describes terms used in the Workato interface and documentation.

- [Install Tealium Audience Discovery for Snowflake](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/snowflake-cloud-data-source/tealium-audience-discovery/install/): Install Tealium Audience Discovery for Snowflake from the Snowflake Marketplace and configure the required roles and permissions.

- [Manage event attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/manage-es-attributes/): This article describes how to manage event attributes.

- [Manage visitor and visit attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/manage-as-attributes/): This article describes how to manage visitor and visit attributes.

- [About AudienceStore and EventStore](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencestore-eventstore/about/): This article describes how to access AudienceStore and EventStore services.

- [About data transformation functions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/data-transformation-functions/about/): This article provides an overview of data transformation functions.

- [About Tealium Insights](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/insights/about/): This article provides information about Tealium Insights, which provides business intelligence capabilities for the Tealium Customer Data Hub.

- [Webhook actions and parameters](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/actions-and-parameters/): This article describes the supported webhook actions and parameters.

- [Add and configure the Tealium Collect tag](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-layer-enrichment/tealium-collect-dle/): This article describes how to configure the Tealium Collect tag to enable data layer enchrichment.

- [Anonymous IDs, user identifiers, and visitor ID attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/anonymous-user-visitor-id-attributes/): This article defines the key components involved in identity resolution: anonymous IDs, user identifiers, and visitor ID attributes.

- [Configure AudienceDB and EventDB](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencedb-eventdb/configure/): This article describes how to configure the attributes to be stored in EventDB and AudienceDB.

- [Connectors interface](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/interface/): The connectors interface provides an intuitive workflow to add, edit, or delete server-side connectors and actions for AudienceStream Customer Data Platform (CDP) and EventStream API Hub.

- [Create a data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/platform-data-sources/create-data-source/): This article describes how to create a data source.

- [Create a function](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/manage-functions/create-function/): This article provides instructions for creating data transformation functions and event or visitor functions.

- [Manage cloud data source error logs](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/error-logs/): This article provides information on viewing, exporting, and downloading cloud data source error log files.

- [Functions code editor](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/code-editor/): This article provides information on the features of the functions code editor.

- [GET Data Layer Enrichment Public API](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-layer-enrichment/data-layer-enrichment-api/get-data-layer-enrichment-api/): GET Data Layer Enrichment Public API lets you get profile data for an active visitor.

- [GET Profile Definition API](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/data-layer-enrichment/data-layer-enrichment-api/get-profile-definition-api/): GET Profile Definition API lets you retrieve audience and badge data from a Tealium profile.

- [Install Tealium Collect](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/tealium-collect/): Learn how to install Tealium Collect.

- [Manage audiences in Tealium Audience Discovery for Snowflake](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/snowflake-cloud-data-source/tealium-audience-discovery/manage/): Create audiences from Snowflake data and manage them for use in Tealium.

- [Manage Moments API engines](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/moments-api/manage/): This article describes how to create and manage Moments API engines.

- [Rate limits](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/rate-limits/): This article describes Data Connect rate limits.

- [Set up a Tealium Connect data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/tealium-connect-data-source/): This article describes how to set up a Tealium Connect data source.

- [Connector template variables](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/templates/template-variables/): Learn how to build advanced connector requests using template variables.

- [Usage examples](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/enrichments/usage-examples/): Increment or decrement number – &amp;ldquo;Lifetime Order Total (LTV)&amp;rdquo; This enrichment can modify a number with a positive value (increment) or a …

- [Event and visitor functions V2](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/v2-functions/): This article provides information on event and visitor functions that use the V2 runtime.

- [View import status](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/file-import/file-transfer-service/view-import-status/): This article describes how to view the status of imported files.

- [APIs and libraries for functions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/modules/): This article provides information on the APIs and libraries available for functions.

- [Data Connect error handling](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/error-handling/): This article provides an overview of error handling and notifications in Data Connect.

- [Data Connect roles](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/roles/): This article provides an overview of Data Connect roles and how to set them for each Tealium profile.

- [Set up a Tealium Connect event specification](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/tealium-connect-event-spec/): This article describes how to create an event specification for use in Tealium Connect.

- [Validate webhook action](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/validate-webhook-action/): This article describes how to validate webhook actions.

- [Functions dashboard](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/dashboard/): This article provides information about the functions dashboard.

- [About templates](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/insights/about-templates/): This article provides information about templates and predefined dashboards.

- [Add a connector](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/add/): This article explains how to add and configure a connector.

- [AudienceDB data guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencedb-eventdb/audiencedb-data-guide/): This article describes the data available within the Tealium DataAccess AudienceDB.

- [Functions best practices](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/best-practices/): This article provides best practices for functions to avoid exceeding the memory and execution time limits.

- [Cloud attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/cloudstream/attributes/): This article provides an overview of how cloud attributes are used and steps to add, duplicate, or remove an attribute.

- [Configure AudienceStore](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencestore-eventstore/configure-audiencestore/): This article provides information about configuring an AudienceStore connector.

- [Connect to a data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/platform-data-sources/connect-to-data-source/): This article describes how to add and connect data sources to your account.

- [Connect Snowflake audiences to Tealium](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/snowflake-cloud-data-source/tealium-audience-discovery/connect-snowflake-audiences/): Connect an audience from Tealium Audience Discovery for Snowflake to Tealium using a Snowflake cloud data source.

- [Moments API endpoint](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/moments-api/endpoint/): This article describes the Moments API endpoint.

- [Event parameter](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/data-transformation-functions/event-parameter/): This article provides information on the `event` parameter for data transformation functions.

- [Event specifications](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/event-health/event-specs/): Event specifications are a way to validate the quality of incoming data.

- [Data Connect events](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/events/): This article describes the Data Connect events format.

- [Template helper functions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/templates/help-functions/): Learn how to build complex connector requests using these helper functions.

- [Visitor ID attribute enrichment for Tealium Connect](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/tealium-connect-visitor-id-attr-enrichment/): This article describes how to map and enrich a visitor ID attribute in AudienceStream for use in Data Connect.

- [Test functions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/manage-functions/test-functions/): This article provides information on testing functions and obtaining data for test payloads.

- [Usage examples](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/usage-examples/): This article provides examples of HTTP requests.

- [Event and visitor functions V3](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/event-visitor-v3/): This article provides information on event and visitor functions that use the V3 runtime.

- [Visitor ID attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/visitor-id-attribute/): This article provides information on visitor ID attributes, best practices for using visitor ID attributes, and when and how to assign a value to them.

- [Changelog](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/webhook-connectors/changelog/): 2024-07-10 Added the following actions to all HTTP-based webhook connectors:
Send Event Data via HTTP Request Send Visitor Data via HTTP Request Send …

- [Data Connect dashboard](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/dashboard/): This article describes the Data Connect dashboard.

- [Manage event specifications](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/event-health/manage-event-specifications/): This article explains how to manage event specifications.

- [Configure EventStore](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencestore-eventstore/configure-eventstore/): This article provides information about enabling an event feed for EventStore.

- [Create a recipe in Data Connect](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/create-recipe/): This article describes how to create a recipe in Data Connect.

- [Delete a function](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/manage-functions/delete-function/): This article provides instructions for deleting a function from the **Functions Overview** page and from the functions code editor.

- [Edit data source settings](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/platform-data-sources/edit-data-source-settings/): This article describes how to add and connect data sources to your Customer Data Hub profile.

- [Event feeds](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/event-health/event-feeds/): Event feeds are used to manage and inspect incoming data. This article shows how to manage event feeds.

- [EventDB data guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencedb-eventdb/eventdb-data-guide/): This guide describes the data available in EventDB, including built-in columns, cookie variables, tag execution data, and performance timing metrics.

- [Example code](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/data-transformation-functions/functions-transforms-example-code/): This article provides code examples for data transformation functions.

- [Flatten nested objects](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/data-transformation-functions/transforms-flatten-library/): This article provides information on the `flatten` library, which is used to convert a nested object into an object that is not nested.

- [Moments API and iQ Tag Management example implementation guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/moments-api/iq-implementation-example/): See an example solution that integrates Moments API into your Tealium iQ Tag Management installation.

- [Manage audiences](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/manage/): This article provides information about creating, editing, and deleting audiences, as well as activating audiences and creating segments.

- [Manage connectors](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/manage/): This article explains how to manage connectors, including activating or deactivating connectors, duplicating and deleting connectors, and getting a connector or action UID.

- [Manage dashboards](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/insights/manage-dashboards/): This article provides information about generating dashboards and updating the templates for dashboards.

- [Migrate a V2 function to the V3 runtime](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/migrate-v2-to-v3/): New event and visitor functions use the **Action V3** runtime. Existing functions that use the **Action V2** runtime need to be migrated to the **Action V3** runtime. This article provides information on the migration process.

- [Manage CloudStream segments](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/cloudstream/segments/): This article provides information about how to manage CloudStream segments.

- [Data Connect usage reports](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/usage-reports/): This article describes the Data Connect usage reports.

- [Visitor stitching example](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/diagram/): This article provides an example of the visitor stitching process that occurs when a visitor uses different devices during a series of visits to a website.

- [Delayed actions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/delayed-actions/): This article provides information about delayed connector actions and how they work.

- [Manage segments](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/manage-segments/): This article provides information about creating, editing, and deleting segments, as well as saving segments as favorites.

- [Threshold alerts](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/insights/threshold-alerts/): Set up threshold alerts to receive email notifications when a dashboard visual crosses a defined value.

- [About restricted data](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/restricted-data/): Restricted data is a property to identify attributes that contain sensitive data.

- [Batched actions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/batched-actions/): This article describes how batched actions work with Tealium connectors.

- [Connect to EventDB and AudienceDB](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencedb-eventdb/connect/): This article describes provides information on connecting to Redshift, getting database credentials, and writing SQL queries.

- [Event and visitor function examples (V2)](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/function-examples-v2/): This article provides example code for V2 event and visitor functions.

- [Moments API Insights](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/moments-api/insights/): This article provides information about the data and visualizations displayed in the Moments API Insights tab.

- [Live events](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/event-health/live-events/): The Live Events chart is used to inspect real-time incoming data. This article shows how to inspect and evaluate data quality.

- [Manage datasets](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/insights/manage-datasets/): This article provides information about creating and deleting datasets and using datasets in an analysis.

- [Manage logs](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/manage-functions/manage-function-logs/): This article provides information on viewing, exporting, and downloading function log files.

- [Manage recipes in Data Connect](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/manage-recipes/): This article describes how to view, edit, start, and stop recipes.

- [View files in AudienceStore and EventStore](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencestore-eventstore/dataaccess-view-files/): This article provides information on how to view files in AudienceStore and EventStore.

- [Visitor stitching guidelines](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/guidelines/): This article provides guidelines for visitor stitching.

- [Connectors dashboard](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/connectors-dashboard/): This article provides information about the connectors dashboard and the visuals it displays.

- [Datasets best practices](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/insights/datasets-best-practices/): This article describes best practices for creating and managing datasets in an analysis.

- [AudienceStore data guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencestore-eventstore/audiencestore-data-guide/): This article is a summary of the visitor-level attributes that are made available through AudienceStore.

- [Create a visitor ID attribute](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/create-a-visitor-id-attribute/): This article provides detailed steps to create a visitor ID attribute. This example uses `customer_id`, which is typically available when a visitor makes a purchase, as a user identifier.

- [Event attributes and enrichments reference](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/reference/event-attributes-and-enrichments-reference/): This article lists all event attributes with their enrichments and corresponding rule conditions.

- [Event and visitor functions examples (V3)](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/v3-function-examples/): This article provides example code for V3 event and visitor functions.

- [Manage analyses](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/insights/manage-analyses/): This article provides information on creating, printing, and publishing an analysis.

- [Manage global variables](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/manage-functions/manage-global-variables/): This article provides information on adding, editing, and deleting global variables that can be used in event, visitor, and data record functions.

- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/troubleshooting-data-connect/): This article describes how to troubleshoot issues in Data Connect.

- [Visit and visitor attributes and enrichments reference](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/attributes/reference/visit-and-visitor-attributes-and-enrichments-reference/): This article lists all visit and visitor attributes with their enrichments.

- [Facebook Lead Ads Data Connect Integration Setup Guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/facebook/): This article describes how to set up an integration between Tealium Data Connect and Facebook Lead Ads.

- [Add authentication to an event, visitor, or data record function](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/authentication/): This article provides information on adding authentication to an event, visitor, or data record function.

- [Authenticom DealerVault Data Connect Integration Setup Guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/authenticom/): This article describes how to set up an integration between Tealium Data Connect and Authenticom DealerVault.

- [DealerTrack Data Connect Integration Setup Guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-connect/manage-data-connect-integrations/dealertrack/): This article describes how to set up an integration between Tealium Data Connect and DealerTrack.

- [Add OAuth 2.0 authentication to an event, visitor, or data record function](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/oauth2-authentication/): This article provides information on adding OAuth 2.0 authentication to an event, visitor, or data record function.

- [EventStore data guide](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/audiencestore-eventstore/eventstore-data-guide/): This article provides a summary of event-level data points made available through EventStore.

- [Healthcare dashboard](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/insights/healthcare-dashboard/): This article provides information about the healthcare dashboard.

- [View function statistics](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/manage-functions/view-function-statistics/): This article provides instructions for viewing throttling information for data transformation functions and execution statistics for all function types.

- [Visitor stitching scenarios](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/scenarios/): This article shows examples of a visitor using a different email address on a subsequent visit to a webiste.

- [About TAPID cookies](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/tapid/): This document explains what the TAPID cookie is and how the Tealium platform uses it.

- [Generate a UID2 with a visitor function](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/uid2/): Learn how to use a visitor function to generate a UID2 for a user and save it in the visitor profile.

- [Use a function to generate an EUID](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/event-visitor-functions/euid/): This article describes how to use a visitor function to generate an EUID for a user and save it in the visitor profile.

- [Single event visitor](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-stitching/single-event-visitor/): This article explains how Tealium handles single event visitors.

- [About Audience Discovery](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/audience-discovery/about/): This article describes the features of Audience Discovery.

- [About server-side saving and publishing](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/save-publish/about/): This article describes the concepts of saving and publishing on the server-side.

- [About audience sizing](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/audience-sizing/about/): This article provides information about audience sizing, audience segments, and jobs.

- [About data record functions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/functions/data-record-functions/data-record-functions/): This article provides an overview of data record functions in CloudStream.

- [Action frequency capping and prioritization](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/frequency-capping/about/): This article describes how to use action frequency capping and prioritization to control when actions trigger and which actions take priority.

- [About labels](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/labels/about/): Labels help you identify data sources, event feeds, event specs, and attributes for your own organization and filtering. This article provides information on labels and how they are useful, and also shows you how to create, manage, search, and filter by labels.

- [About server-side profiles](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/profiles/about/): This article describes profiles in your server-side account.

- [About Trace](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/trace/about/): Trace is a testing and inspection tool for your server-side configuration that tracks your workflow to show how events and customer data are processed in real-time.

- [Databricks cloud data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/databricks/): This article describes how to set up the Databricks cloud data source.

- [Amazon Redshift cloud data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/cloud-data/amazon-redshift/): This article describes how to set up the Amazon Redshift cloud data source.

- [Data sources for iQ Tag Management](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/data-sources-for-iq-tag-management/): This article explains how to use data sources with existing iQ Tag Management accounts.

- [Import data using Visitor Data Enrichment (/vdata)](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/vdata-endpoint/): Send real-time data updates to EventStream from any external system.

- [Manage traces](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/trace/manage/): This article describes how to start and stop a new trace or join an active trace.

- [View a trace](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/trace/view/): This article describes how to use the overview panel and view the trace log.

- [Visitor identification using Tealium data sources](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-sources/visitor-identification/): This article describes how visitor identification works for data you upload using a Tealium data source.

- [About DataAccess](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/about/): This article describes Tealium DataAccess, it's capabilities, and the different data formats.

- [Cooldown groups](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/frequency-capping/cooldown/): This article describes how to use cooldown groups in frequency capping.

- [Manage Audience Sizing](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/audience-sizing/manage/): This article explains how to manage Audience Sizing.

- [Manage labels](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/labels/manage/): This article explains how to manage labels.

- [Moments API managed MCP server](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/moments-api/managed-mcp-server/): Learn about the Moments API managed MCP server.

- [Server-side save and publish workflow](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/save-publish/save-publish/): This article describes how to save and publish changes to your server-side configuration.

- [About Predict](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/getting-started/about-predict/): This article describes the Tealium Predict ML product and how it is used to create, train, and deploy machine learning models to make predictions about visitor behavior.

- [Using Predict](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/getting-started/using-predict/): This article provides an overview of the Tealium Predict ML workflow, basic Predict implementation, and best practices for readying your data and creating models.

- [Best practices](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/getting-started/best-practices/): This article lists best practices that can help you get started selecting your target attribute, readying your data, and using the models that you create with Predict ML.

- [Prerequisites](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/getting-started/prerequisites/): This article describes what is required to use the Tealium Predict ML product, suggested steps to take before you begin to ensure ideal results, and available services.

- [Terminology](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/getting-started/terminology/): This article defines general statistical modeling terminology, terms specific to Tealium products, and terms used in the Tealium Predict ML interface.

- [Manage Audience Discovery](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/audience-discovery/manage/): This article explains how to add, edit, delete, and view audience filters in Audience Discovery.

- [Profile switcher](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/profiles/ss-profile-switcher/): This article explains how to use the server-side profile switcher.

- [Audience Sizing examples](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/audience-sizing/examples/): This article provides examples of the Audience Sizing feature.

- [Concurrent users](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/save-publish/concurrent-users/): This article explains how the platform manages changes from concurrent users.

- [Frequency capping example](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/connectors/frequency-capping/example/): This article provides a usage example of frequency capping.

- [Use labels](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/labels/use/): This article provides information on how to use labels.

- [Attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/attributes/): The first step in using AudienceStream is to create attributes. Attributes allow you to define the important characteristics that represent a visitor's habits, preferences, actions, and engagement with your brand. This section will describe the two types of attributes and the different kinds of data they can store.

- [Data sources](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/data-sources/): A data source is any system that sends data to Tealium EventStream. These systems include websites, native mobile apps, or any custom application that can report event data.

- [Data sources: Add a data source](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/add-data-source/): This step uses the HTTP API data source as an example, but you can use any platform for which you have access to the code.

- [Data Sources: Install and Test](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/install-test/): Installing and testing the HTTP API is as simple as entering a URL into a browser. If you chose a different platform for your data source, go ahead and add the necessary code according to the installation instructions.

- [Attributes Enrichments](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/enrichments/): After you identify the attributes you want, you will configure them with enrichments.

- [Decide what to predict](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/strategy/decide-what-to-predict/): This article describes attributes to target, exclude, and output in Tealium Predict ML.

- [Attribute readiness](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/strategy/attribute-readiness/): This article describes how to select the right target attribute to use in your models.

- [Prepare your data](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/strategy/prepare-your-data/): This article describes data wellness concepts and actionable steps you can take to examine and optimize the readiness of your data layer before starting with Tealium Predict ML.

- [Attributes Badges](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/badges/): This document explains visitor attributes badges.

- [Attributes Offline Data (Omnichannel)](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/offline-data/): While AudienceStream primarily acts on real-time customers of your digital properties, it can also work with offline data through omnichannel imports. You can take offline data, such as in-store sales or call center activity, and correlate it to your online visitor data to complete the customer data profile.

- [Preloaded attributes](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/preloaded/): This document explains preloaded attributes.

- [Concurrency best practices](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/save-publish/best-practices/): This article explains best practices for team collaboration.

- [Favorite numbers](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/audience-discovery/favorite-numbers/): This article explains how to use favorite numbers with audiences.

- [Live Events](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/live-events/): Use Live Events to manage and inspect incoming events in real-time. You'll use this to verify that the test events from your data source are received.

- [Visitor Stitching](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/visitor-stitching/): Now that you've seen how to build rich customer profiles using attributes and enrichments, it's time to review how to identify your customers through identity resolution and visitor stitching.

- [Add a model](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/training-models/add-a-model/): This article describes how to add a model, select a target attribute, an output attribute, and exclude attributes from a model.

- [Visitor Stitching Visitor ID](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/visitor-stitching-visitor-id/): The visitor ID attribute is used to identify customers using a value that is guaranteed to be unique to each customer.

- [Review your model](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/training-models/review-your-model/): This article describes optional review steps and how to initiate the first training for your model.

- [Visitor ID Part 1](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/visitor-id-part-1/): In this step, we will show how to safely create a visitor ID attribute and introduce Audience Discovery, which allows us to validate the identity resolution strategy before finalizing it.

- [Start training](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/strategy/start-training/): This article describes the steps required to start training your model.

- [Visitor ID Discovery](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/visitor-id-discovery/): This is a critical step to ensure that our identity resolution strategy is accurate and that the attribute we've chosen, Email Address, is unique across our customer base.

- [Visitor ID Part 2](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/visitor-id-part-2/): In this step we will complete the identity resolution plan by creating the visitor ID attribute for Email Address and populate it with the values we are already collecting in the corresponding string attribute.

- [Server-side order of operations](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/order-of-operations/): This article describes the order of operations for server-side data collection and covers the following processes:  incoming events, enriching attributes, processing visitor profiles,  building audiences, and triggering connectors.

- [Server-side version history](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/save-publish/version-history/): This article explains how to use the version history to display and compare server-side versions and revisions.

- [Audiences](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/audiences/): This document provides information about audiences and describes how to create an audience.

- [Event specifications](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/event-spec/): Event specifications represent your data layer in EventStream and provide a way to organize and validate the quality of incoming data.

- [Event Specifications: Add a Spec](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/add-spec/): Add event specifications directly from the event details view in Live Events.

- [Event Specifications: Data Quality](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/data-quality/): After you define event specifications, Live Events displays the quality of your incoming data.

- [Evaluate trained models](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/evaluate-models/evaluate-trained-models/): This article provides an overview of how to evaluate your trained version before deploying your model. Use the following sections as a guide to view model status and the potential strength of predictions for your model before you deploy.

- [Strength scores](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/evaluate-models/strength-scores/): This article provides detailed information about model scoring techniques and formulas used to assign scores and ratings to trained and deployed models in Tealium Predict ML.

- [The Confusion Matrix](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/evaluate-models/the-confusion-matrix/): This article describes the Confusion Matrix and how to use it to evaluate a trained model.

- [The ROC/AUC curve](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/evaluate-models/the-roc-auc-curve/): This article describes the ROC/AUC curve and how to use it as a performance measurement of a trained model.

- [Probability distribution](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/evaluate-models/probability-distribution/): This article describes how to use the probability distribution to interpret trained models.

- [View model status](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/evaluate-models/view-model-status/): This article describes how to view your model status.

- [Retrain a model](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/evaluate-models/retrain-a-model/): This article describes how to retrain a model after evaluating the model and determining changes that are required to improve predictions.

- [Live Visitors (formerly Visitor profile sampler)](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-profile-sampler/): This article describes the features of the visitor profile sampler, which provides an overview of the device, badge, and audience activity from your visitors.

- [Visitor Search (formerly Visitor lookup tool)](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/visitor-search/): This article explains the visitor search tool, which is a graphical interface of the visitor privacy API. A visitor search uses a visitor ID attribute and a search value to locate a visitor record in the system.

- [Rule Condition Definitions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/audiences/rule-conditions/): This article lists the operators available when building conditions for audiences and rules.

- [Connectors](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/connectors/): This document explains connectors in AudienceStream.

- [Event feeds](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/event-feeds/): Event feeds are a collection of events that match specific conditions based on their attributes.

- [Add an Event Feed](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/add-feed/): This step shows how to create an event feed to capture search events that contain zero results.

- [Deploy a model](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/deploy/deploy-a-model/): This article describes how to deploy one or more versions of a trained model.

- [Undeploy a model](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/deploy/undeploy-a-model/): This article describes how to undeploy one or more versions of a trained model.

- [Connectors: Add a connector](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/add-connector/): This step shows how to add a connector, configure an action, and use data mappings.

- [Deployed model health](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/deploy/deployed-model-health/): This article provides explanations of the metrics and ratings available when using Tealium Predict ML that help you understand the quality of your deployed models.

- [Model retraining recommendations](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/deploy/model-retraining-recommendations/): This article provides best practices and recommendations for retraining a model.

- [Delete a model](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/deploy/delete-a-model/): This article describes how to delete a model. You cannot delete a version of a model, only an entire model.

- [Connectors](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/connectors/): A connector is an integration between Tealium and another vendor that is used to transmit data. A connector contains actions that represent the vendor's supported APIs.

- [Trace](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/trace/): Trace is the testing and validation tool for AudienceStream.

- [Connectors: Add a connector](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/add-connector/): This step shows how to add a connector, configure an action, and use data mappings.

- [Audience considerations](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/audiences/audience-considerations/): This article serves as a guideline of items to consider when creating audiences using results from Tealium Predict.

- [Trace: Test with Trace](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/test-trace/): In this step, you will use Trace to validate that all of the previous configuration is working as expected. You will get a trace ID, add it to your application (or use the Tealium Tool), perform a simple test, then inspect the logs in the Trace interface.

- [Create audiences with Predict](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/audiences/enrich-your-output-attribute/): This article describes how to use your predictions to create one or more audiences.

- [Save and Publish](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/save-publish/): Before we finish, it is important to understand how the save and publish process works and how to navigate your version history. This section will explain the difference between Save, Save as, and Publish and how to view your version history.

- [Trace](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/trace/): Trace is the testing and validation tool for EventStream. Use Trace to validate that your configuration is working properly and that connector actions are triggered.

- [Trace: Test with Trace](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/test-trace/): In this step, use Trace to validate that your configuration is working as expected.

- [Next Steps](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/audiencestream-cdp/next-steps/): Now that you have a basic understanding of how Tealium AudienceStream works, you are ready to move on.

- [Save and publish](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/save-publish/): This section explains the save and publish process and how to navigate your version history.

- [Machine learning vs. artificial intelligence](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/advanced/machine-learning-vs-artificial-intelligence/): This article provides a generic overview of the differences between machine learning and artificial intelligence.

- [Machine learning concepts and technology](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/predict/advanced/machine-learning-concepts-and-technology/): This article describes Machine Learning technology concepts, goals, audiences, and technological advances.

- [About server-side permissions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/settings/user-permissions/about/): This article describes how to manage server-side users and permission roles for the Tealium Customer Data Hub.

- [About testing](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/save-publish/test/): This article contains notes about the publication process on the server side of the Tealium platform.

- [Connecting SQL Workbench/J](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/data-viz-tools/connecting-sql-workbench/): This guide describes how to download and install SQL Workbench/J and connect to the database associated with your DataAccess account.

- [Data Supply Chain dashboard](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/data-supply-chain-dashboard/): The data supply chain dashboard provides a comprehensive summary of the data supply chain of the Customer Data Hub.

- [Next steps](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/getting-started/eventstream-api-hub/next-steps/): Now that you have a basic understanding of how Tealium EventStream works, you are ready to move on.

- [Server-side account settings](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/settings/account-settings/): The following article describes all Customer Data Hub profile settings.

- [Connecting Snowflake](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/data-viz-tools/connecting-snowflake/): This article describes how to load Tealium data into Snowflake using the Snowflake web interface.

- [Integrate with Tealium iQ](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/save-publish/with-tealium-iq/): This article describes the process of publishing on the server side of the Tealium platform.

- [Connecting Tableau](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/data-viz-tools/connecting-tableau/): This article describes how to connect Tableau to Tealium DataAccess

- [Manage server-side user permissions](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/settings/user-permissions/manage/): This article explains how to manage user permissions.

- [Connecting Microsoft Power BI](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/data-storage/data-viz-tools/connecting-microsoft-power-bi/): This article describes how to connect Microsoft Power BI to Tealium DataAccess

- [Managing server-side user permissions FAQ](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/settings/user-permissions/faq/): This article describes how to manage server-side users and permission roles for the Tealium Customer Data Hub.

- [Content Security Policy](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/settings/tealium-content-security-policies-reference-guide/): This article lists the domains you need to add to your Content Security Policy (CSP).

- [Server-side bot filtering](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/settings/server-side-bot-filtering/): This article describes how Tealium filters out bot traffic in server-side environments.

- [Understanding usage reports](https://docs.tealium.com/server-side/usage-report/): This article describes usage reports and how to access them.
